steps.title: Substitute the known y-value
steps.content:
The second equation gives $y=28$. Substitute that into the first equation:
$$
28=12x-20
$$

steps.title: Solve for the x-value
steps.content:
Add $20$ to both sides, then divide by $12$:
$$
48=12x
$$
$$
4=x
$$
So the solution has coordinates $(4,28)$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two equations and use their intersection point. The intersection is $(4,28)$. The answer is A.
